methods.description: Process for collecting Ch. luridus larvae and preparing them for cytogenetic analysis.

steps.description: Ch. luridus larvae of the last (fourth) instar were used in the work. The collection sites and sample sizes are listed in Table 1.
steps.searchable_summary: collect Ch. luridus larvae and record sample sizes

steps.description: The larvae were fixed in a mixture of 96% ethanol and glacial acetic acid (3:1) and stored in a refrigerator.
steps.searchable_summary: fix larvae in ethanol and acetic acid mixture

methods.description: Preparation of squash preparations of salivary gland polytene chromosomes and mapping of chromosome arms.

steps.description: Squash preparations of the salivary gland polytene chromosomes were made conventionally, using aceto-orcein staining.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare squash preparations using aceto-orcein staining

steps.description: Polytene chromosome arms A, E, and F were mapped according to Keyl (1962) and arms C and D, according to Dévai et al. (1989), using the banding sequences of Chironomus piger Strenzke, 1959 polytene chromosomes as a standard.
steps.searchable_summary: map chromosome arms using standard banding sequences

methods.description: Analysis of chromosomal polymorphisms and genotypic combinations in populations.

steps.description: The following cytogenetic characteristics of chromosomal polymorphisms in populations were used: the set, number and frequency of banding sequences and their genotypic combinations, the percent of the larvae with heterozygous inversions, and the mean number of heterozygous inversions per individual.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze chromosomal polymorphisms and genotypic combinations
